B1528 SIROCCO · Issue #2760

B1528 SIROCCO current/rudder force review — Issue #2760

This interactive report visualizes rudder-induced, OCIMF reference hull-current review, and summed total force/yaw-moment components for B1528 SIROCCO current-heading/rudder combinations with rudder angles from 0 deg through +28 deg. Component sums are comparison loads only; this is not a validated whole-vessel current-load, mooring, tug, bank-effect, or compliance model.

SIROCCO issue #2760 current/rudder force-component review at COG; rudder-induced ship-fixed X/Y/N components are derived by rotating local current-frame loads by heading offset, and generic/reference OCIMF tanker-current review loads are estimated separately from the licensed off-repo workbook route; bank effect, tug loads, mooring-line stiffness, current-profile variation, propeller race, and class compliance conclusion are excluded

Default speed policy: 3.08 kn is the issue #2760 default current speed; practical plots/tables are bounded to 0..4 kn.

Input data

Key input values used by both the interactive HTML and print/PDF report views.

LBP225.500 m
Beam32.260 m
Draft12.200 m
Current frontal projected area393.572 m²
Current lateral projected area2751.100 m²
Yaw lever135.300 m
Rudder area44.939563 m²
Rudder span9.000 m
Ship speed over ground0.000 kn
Water density1025.0 kg/m³
Barrass/workbook β600.0
Propeller rotation factor Cr1.0
Current speed sweep0, 1, 2, 3, 3.08, 4 kn + chart-default 3.08 kn
Heading/rudder grid-5..5 deg heading, 1 deg step × 0..28 deg rudder, 2 deg step

Sample calculation

Representative hand-check point: issue #2760 default 3.08 kn, heading +5 deg, rudder +28 deg, Cr=1.0.

  1. V = 3.08 kn × 0.51444 = 1.58448 m/s; issue default speed.
  2. α = δ - ψ = 28.0 deg - 5.0 deg = 23.0 deg.
  3. F = 600.0 × 44.939563 × 1.58448² × 1.0 = 67694.127 N.
  4. Fn = F × sin(alpha) = 26450.202 N.
  5. X_ship = 8173.562 N, Y_ship = 25155.637 N, N_ship = 3403.557744 kN-m.
  6. q = 0.5 × rho × V²; current coefficients use report-specific OCIMF loaded-tanker workbook interpolation tied to the approved provenance route.
  7. X_current = -16548.971 N, Y_current = 120548.359 N, N_current = 6724.854215 kN-m; totals are component sums at COG.

Ship/current/rudder geometry schematic

This schematic updates with the current speed/rudder controls and uses the selected breakdown heading. It shows ship-fixed axes, the current heading offset ψ, rudder command δ, and effective rudder inflow angle α = δ - ψ. Positive bow-to-port convention applies to ψ, δ, Y_ship, and yaw moment.

  • Black dashed axes: ship-fixed COG frame; +X forward and +Y port.
  • Blue vector: current heading offset ψ relative to ship centerline.
  • Orange vector: selected rudder command δ; the selector intentionally controls this angle for Chart 1/3/4 review.
  • Green vector: selected-case total X/Y force direction from current + rudder components.
  • Effective inflow: α = δ - ψ; when alpha is zero, rudder-induced load is zero but hull-current load remains.

Controls

Selected-speed envelope summary

Envelope values below update with the current-speed selector and use the full heading × rudder grid at that speed. The selected default is 3.08 kn; 4 kn is only the upper bound of the plotted/table range.

Chart 1 — Rudder-induced ship-fixed force components over heading

Shows rudder-induced X_ship, Y_ship, in kN for the selected current speed and rudder angle. All rudder angles are selectable; the default rudder angle is 28.0 deg port.

Chart 2 — Rudder-induced ship-fixed yaw moment chart over heading × rudder

Shows signed rudder-induced N_ship yaw moment (kN-m) over heading offset and rudder angle for the selected current speed.

Chart 3 — Generic OCIMF current vs rudder vs total Y force

Compares ship-fixed Y_ship force from the first-cut generic/reference OCIMF tanker-current review estimate, the rudder-induced force, and their component sum for the selected speed and rudder angle. Current coefficients are tied to the approved licensed off-repo workbook route, not vessel-specific SIROCCO curves.

Chart 4 — Generic OCIMF current vs rudder vs total yaw moment about COG

Compares signed yaw moment about COG from the generic/reference OCIMF current-review component, the rudder-induced component, and the summed total yaw moment. Positive yaw moment is bow-to-port.

Selected-case force breakdown

Updates with current speed and rudder angle. Values are shown at the heading with maximum absolute total yaw moment for the selected trace, so individual X/Y/N component paths can be reviewed together.

ComponentX ship (kN)Y ship port (kN)Yaw moment about COG (kN-m)
OCIMF current———
Rudder induced———
Total / mooring reaction opposite———

—

Method and provenance

V=kn*0.51444; F=beta*A_R*V^2*Cr; alpha=delta-psi; X_local=F*sin(alpha)^2; Y_local=F*sin(alpha)*cos(alpha); X_ship=X_local*cos(psi)-Y_local*sin(psi); Y_ship=X_local*sin(psi)+Y_local*cos(psi); N=Y_ship*yaw_lever
  • Heading/rudder effective-angle convention: α = rudder_angle_deg - heading_offset_deg.
  • Local-to-ship transform rotates local current-frame X/Y loads into ship-fixed COG axes.
  • Current review equations: q=0.5*rho*V²; Cxc, Cyc, and Cxyc are interpolated from OCIMF loaded-tanker A9/A10/A11 workbook curves through the approved off-repo source route; X_current=q*(beam*draft)*Cxc; Y_current=q*(LBP*draft)*Cyc; N_current=q*(LBP*draft)*LBP*Cxyc.
  • When rudder_angle_deg equals heading_offset_deg, alpha is zero and this rudder-induced component is zero; that is not total hull current load.
  • Scope note: hull current loads are first-cut generic/reference OCIMF tanker-current comparison loads; this is not a validated whole-vessel current-load or oblique-current hull/rudder interaction model.
